en: Electrical Safety

Electrical work may only be carried out by qualified electricians or, where permitted, by instructed persons under the supervision of a qualified electrician. Persons performing electrical work must possess the necessary training, knowledge, and experience to avoid electrical hazards.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 1(3)(1,2), 12(2)
Qualified Electrical Personnel

Electrical equipment and installations must be suitable for the intended operational use and environmental conditions, including mechanical, thermal, and chemical influences.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 2(2)
Equipment Suitability

Live parts must be insulated, enclosed, or otherwise protected to prevent direct contact. Adjacent live parts must be covered or safeguarded where work is performed nearby.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 3(1), 12(1)(5)
Live Parts Protected

Electrical installations must include at least one effective fault protection measure to prevent danger from indirect contact in the event of a fault.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 4(1), 12(1)(4)
Fault Protection Implemented

Electrical installations and equipment may only be used after required inspections have been completed. Construction site electrical installations and equipment must be inspected at least weekly for visible defects. Mining electrical safety devices must be inspected at the prescribed weekly and monthly intervals. Identified defects must be rectified before continued use.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 7(1)(1), 7(3)(2), 7(3)(3), 9(1)(1-3)\nWork Equipment Ordinance, Section 14
Inspections (Before Use, Periodic)

Electrical installations and equipment must be tested before commissioning and after modification or repair. Testing must be carried out by qualified electricians and must verify basic protection against direct contact, fault protection against indirect contact, and the effectiveness of additional protective measures.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 7(1)(2), 7(3)(1), 10(1)(2-4)
Testing (Commissioning and After Modification)
The work area must be clearly defined before de-energized work begins. Circuits must be disconnected before de-energized work commences.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 12(1)
De-Energized Work (Pre-Work)

Personnel performing live work must have specialized training and receive refresher courses. Employees must receive instruction on storage, care, and maintenance of live work PPE. Employees must receive thorough instruction on required safety distances near live parts.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 13(2)(1), 13(6), 14(5)
Training

Necessary tools and PPE must be available for live work. Live parts must be protected by covers, enclosures or insulation where feasible. A safety distance larger than the danger zone must be determined Pre-Work begins.\nElectrical Safety Ordinance, Section 13(2)(2), 13(3), 14(3), 14(4)(1-2)
Control Measures (Engineering, Administrative, and/or Personal Protective Equipment)
